name: io.github.Sowiedu/edict
description: AI-agent programming language. JSON AST in, WASM out. Typed, effect-tracked, Z3-verified.

tools.name: edict_schema
tools.description: Return the JSON Schema defining valid Edict AST programs. Use format 'minimal' for reduced token cost (strips descriptions).

tools.name: edict_version
tools.description: Return compiler version and capability info.

tools.name: edict_examples
tools.description: Return example programs as JSON ASTs.

tools.name: edict_validate
tools.description: Validate an Edict AST against the compiler's JSON schema without typing or compiling. Use this as a first pass.

tools.name: edict_check
tools.description: Run the full semantic checker (name resolution, type checking, effect checking, contract verification) on an AST. Supports single module or multi-module input.

tools.name: edict_compile
tools.description: Compile a semantically valid Edict AST into a WebAssembly module. Returns the WASM binary encoded as a base64 string. Supports single module or multi-module input.

tools.name: edict_run
tools.description: Execute a compiled WebAssembly module (provided as base64) in a sandboxed runtime with configurable limits.

tools.name: edict_patch
tools.description: Apply surgical patches to an Edict AST by nodeId, then run the full check pipeline. Fix errors without resubmitting the entire AST.

tools.name: edict_errors
tools.description: Return machine-readable catalog of all structured error types.

tools.name: edict_lint
tools.description: Run non-blocking lint analysis on an Edict AST. Returns quality warnings without blocking compilation.

tools.name: edict_debug
tools.description: Execute an Edict program with debug instrumentation. Returns structured crash diagnostics including call stack, crash location with nodeId, and step count.

tools.name: edict_compose
tools.description: Compose multiple Edict program fragments into a single module. Fragments declare what they provide and require.

tools.name: edict_explain
tools.description: Given a structured error, returns enriched repair context: pipeline stage, field metadata, example ASTs, and repair strategy.

tools.name: edict_export
tools.description: Export an Edict AST as a portable WASM skill package with validation and manifest generation.

tools.name: edict_import_skill
tools.description: Import and execute a compiled Edict WASM skill package, validating its checksum.

tools.name: edict_generate_tests
tools.description: Auto-generate structured test cases from Z3-verified contracts. Extracts boundary values and counterexamples as regression tests.

tools.name: edict_replay
tools.description: Re-execute a WASM module using a previously recorded replay token for deterministic reproduction of runtime behavior.

tools.name: edict_support
tools.description: Returns structured sponsorship and support information for the Edict project.

prompts.name: write_program
prompts.description: System prompt for writing a new Edict program from a task description. Includes minimal schema, example, and builtin list.

prompts.name: fix_error
prompts.description: Prompt for fixing a structured Edict compiler error. Includes error taxonomy and fix strategy.

prompts.name: add_contracts
prompts.description: Prompt for adding pre/postcondition contracts to existing Edict functions for Z3 formal verification.

prompts.name: review_ast
prompts.description: Prompt for reviewing an Edict AST for quality issues (unused variables, missing effects, dead code, etc.).

resources.description: The full JSON Schema defining valid Edict AST programs.
All example programs as JSON ASTs.
Machine-readable catalog of all structured error types.
JSON Schema for the AST patch protocol.
Sponsorship and support information for the Edict project.
